essay_text:
Every day, I wake up to a frenzy of sounds. From downstairs, I hear my father jabbering away in rapid-fire spurts
of booming Mandarin, my mother answering him in the open, sing-song cadences of Korean, at which point my
twin enters their conversation in crisp, casual Americanisms. They understand one another clearly, correctly. The
subject of their exchange concerns perhaps the most mundane of topics: breakfast.
W
To an outsider, the simultaneous bombardment of different languages might have been disorienting. Yet, in its
jarring dissonance, I find a warring peace. Being raised in such a multilingual environment – in which each spoken
language boasts its own unique brand of inflexions, lilts and pulses – has fostered in me an appreciation for the
way words are pronounced and sounded out. My ears have become crucial to the decipherment of disparate
E
sounds, my tongue and lips to vocal utterances in the vernacular. My multilingual upbringing would meet its match
in another vibrantly aural world: the language of poetry, plays and prose.
I
In the realm of literature, I find home base V– from the sombrely plodding rhythm of Edgar Allan Poe’s short stories
to the mellow euphony of Shakespearean love sonnets to the literal strands of melody intertwined in Tennessee
Williams’ play ‘A Streetcar Named Desire.’ The musicality of the written word oozes off every page, every
inscription: ears cocked, I marvel at its simplicity, delight in its variety, hungrily savouring each and every syllable,
E
plosive, vowel and fricative. Now, my appetite for the literary soundscape turns outwards. My imagination satiated
and spurred, I channel my creative energy into composition, submitting to literary magazines while running a
poetry blog of my own. I create almost as much as I take in.
R
Simultaneously, my love of sound leads me to the stately realm of classical music. At the age of 11, I discover
Chee Yun Kim (a South Korean violinist) to whose luscious timbre my ears grew attuned. Inspired, I take up the
P
fiddle, which broadens my horizons to further uncharted territory – a kingdom in which the haunting melancholia of
Chopin runs deep, and Tchaikovsky’s waltzes dance lightly. As I delve into Baroque, Classical, Romantic and
modern pieces, the grating noises that I initially drew across the strings slowly relax, smoothening in texture, finally
coming alive and taking up a pulse of their own. My violin becomes a new voice – another language thrown into
the cacophony that is my multilingual upbringing, another vehicle for expression and catharsis. I become both the
creator and manipulator of sound.
My two realities are united by the bridge of sound. I realise that it is the soundscape that defines me, pervades my
life and more profoundly, explicates the nexus between literature and music. It is sound that wields the power to
bridge the gulf between people of all walks of life. Poetry readings, non-fiction story telling events, and live plays
bring communities closer through the dynamic exchange of the spoken word, while musicians come together in
chamber ensembles, symphony orchestras and concert bands, each individual a page in a book that makes music
whole.
8
I aspire to reconcile literature and music further: examining the literary significance of Stephen Sondheim’s lyrics
to ‘West Side Story,’ sculpting language befitting John Keats’ poetics, trying my hand in studying the linguistics
behind Tolkien’s ‘Quenya,’ and one day pioneering a literary genre where one writes conventional literature for
which music is composed as either the focus or a sort of accompaniment—a reversal and more musical form of
ekphrasis, perhaps. I am eager to explore the soundscape in all its aspects, in all its forms.
My world is one in which the utterances of Chinese, Korean and English clash in perfect harmony. It is the
wonders of Poe and Shakespeare, Chopin and Tchaikovsky. As I stand upon the bridge between my two realities,
a quietude settles my mind.
W
I am in balance.
E
I
V
E
R
P
9
prompt: Some students have a background, identity, interest, or talent that is so meaningful they believe their application would be incomplete without it. If this sounds like you, then please share your story.
persona_prompt: You are a writer who crafts essays with a rich, sensory tapestry, particularly attuned to auditory details and the musicality of language. Employ sophisticated vocabulary and varied sentence structures, often building from personal anecdotes to broader, philosophical reflections. Utilize vivid imagery, metaphors, and strong verbs to convey depth and appreciation for complex concepts. Aim for a tone that is both analytical and deeply personal, showcasing a nuanced understanding of interconnected artistic realms.
sentence_style: Predominantly complex and elongated sentences, rich in descriptive clauses and evocative adjectives, balanced by occasional short, declarative statements for emphasis. Exhibits a flowing, rhythmic quality, often employing parallel structures to build momentum and highlight connections.
vocabulary_level: Sophisticated — characterized by a precise, elevated lexicon that includes specialized terminology (e.g., "plosive," "fricative," "euphony," "ekphrasis") and rich descriptive adjectives, used without pretension.
structural_patterns: Begins with a specific, sensory-rich personal anecdote, then expands into broader intellectual explorations (literature, music). Ideas are connected through a central unifying metaphor (sound/bridge), leading to a synthesis of personal experience and intellectual aspiration. The essay employs a somewhat circular structure, returning to the initial theme of harmonious dissonance.
distinctive_moves: Employing a central unifying metaphor to connect disparate concepts.
Progression from personal anecdote to abstract intellectual inquiry.
Use of evocative lists and parallel structures for emphasis and rhythm.
Embracing and synthesizing apparent paradoxes (e.g., "warring peace").
themes_and_preoccupations: The transformative power and beauty of sound and language.
The synthesis of diverse cultural and artistic influences.
The interconnectedness of literature and music.
The exploration of personal identity through sensory experience.
self_presentation: Analytical and perceptive, demonstrating a confident and sophisticated understanding of complex interdisciplinary connections. Presents as deeply reflective and appreciative of artistic nuance, while also ambitious in their intellectual pursuits.
cultural_or_contextual_markers: Raised in a multilingual household (Mandarin, Korean, English), fostering an acute awareness of linguistic nuances. Deeply engaged with both Western classical music and a broad literary canon.
avoid_in_imitation: Absence of sensory details, particularly auditory imagery.
Disconnect between personal experience and broader intellectual concepts.
sample_sentence: From the gentle rustle of turning pages to the punctuated silence between orchestral movements, my world is an intricate score, each note an invitation to a deeper understanding of human expression.
